rulings.Case Title: JAMES BARAGAR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Cummins Engine Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2, 4, 5 and 6 (negligence, strict liability and duty to warn) and taken off calendar as to issues 3, 7 and 8 (false representation and punitive damages). Plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable whether Mr. Baragar was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (engine gaskets) attributable to Cummins. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiffs¿ exhibit F (Cummins documents) and H (Cummins¿ interrogatory responses) are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Sheldon¿s declaration are overruled, except the objections to ¶¶ 14 & 15, which are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Sheldon¿s deposition testimony are sustained. Defendant has not sufficiently articulated what is covered by issue 6 to permit adjudication of that issue in its favor. Moreover, it appears that issue 6 seeks adjudication of less than an entire claim Plaintiffs¿ dismissal of its false representation and punitive damages claims render adjudication of those claims unnecessary. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS BRUNELLI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc. F/K/A SQUARE D COMPANY¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff presented insufficient evidence to create a triable fact regarding asbestos content of any product attributable to defendant that the plaintiff was exposed to. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Germain, and attached documents, are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD SILVA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Intervenor Fireman¿s Fund Insurance Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create any triable issue regarding breach of a duty based in negligence law and asbestos content of the acoustic ceiling. Plaintiff provides no authority that the seller of a house is liable in negligence for asbestos-containing acoustic tile in the house where there is no evidence that the seller participated in the installation or selection of the tile and/or failed to make required disclosures. Defendant¿s objection to plaintiff¿s exhibit E is sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD MELLO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Plant Products & Supply Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to submit evidence showing a triable issue as to whether he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (insulation) attributable to defendant at the NASCO site. = (220/HEK)
